summary: High‑quality audience FOB captures a legendary NYE ’87 show highlighted by Terrapin, I Know You Rider and Bird Song with solid Jerry Garcia work.
review: The recording is an audience/front‑of‑house tape that most reviewers describe as clear and well balanced – a good substitute for the original FM mix.  Musically the concert is widely praised for its powerful first set, standout performances of Terrapin Station (Jerry’s churning guitar), a superb I Know You Rider with Neville‑brother backing, and an emotional Bird Song/Other One stretch, though some feel the middle portion drags and note the missing "Promised Land" on the DVD release.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars, highlighting exceptional performances and a good‑quality audience recording; a few mixed comments lower the average slightly.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as a high‑quality FOB (front‑of‑house) tape, generally crisp with a rating around 3.5‑4/5; no major dropouts mentioned, though it’s not a pure soundboard source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong, classic first set (Bertha opener, Bird Song), solid second set though some feel the Over‑played "One>Rat>Throw>Away" block drags; third set peaks with a legendary I Know You Rider backed by the Neville brothers.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia delivers a growling, churning guitar on Terrapin and belts out fast‑paced lyrics on I Know You Rider; also features in funny "Ask the Band" moments.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming praised as kick‑ass on I Know You Rider, especially with Neville brother support.
